colleges.name: UC Davis
colleges.transit: Unitrans buses, free with student ID
colleges.cost: Moderate rent, no car needed ever
colleges.blurb: America's #1 bike city; almost nobody here owns a car.

colleges.name: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
colleges.transit: MTD buses free with student ID, very frequent
colleges.blurb: Free unlimited bus pass plus flat terrain make car-free easy and cheap.

colleges.name: Portland State University
colleges.transit: TriMet light rail and streetcar run through campus
colleges.cost: Higher rent, offset by skipping car costs entirely
colleges.blurb: Light rail runs straight through campus; many students never own a car.

colleges.name: University of Washington
colleges.transit: Link light rail station on campus, U-Pass included in fees
colleges.cost: High rent, but transit is excellent
colleges.blurb: Light rail station on campus plus a universal U-Pass; rent is the main catch.

colleges.name: University of Wisconsin-Madison
colleges.transit: Madison Metro plus a free student bus pass
colleges.blurb: Compact isthmus campus, a strong bus network, and bike paths everywhere.

colleges.name: Cornell University
colleges.transit: TCAT buses free for every student, frequent
colleges.cost: Moderate, well below NYC or Boston
colleges.blurb: Free TCAT bus pass for every student and a small walkable downtown.

colleges.name: University of Oregon
colleges.transit: LTD buses free with student ID
colleges.cost: Moderate, below the West Coast average
colleges.blurb: Eugene's flat grid and free bus pass make a car genuinely unnecessary.

colleges.name: University of Minnesota
colleges.walkability: Connected by skyways and bike paths
colleges.transit: Light rail plus Metro Transit, discounted U-Pass
colleges.blurb: Light rail links both campuses; one of the best bike cities in the US.

colleges.name: University of Pittsburgh / CMU
colleges.transit: Port Authority buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Free transit pass and some of the lowest rent of any major college city.

colleges.name: Arizona State University
colleges.transit: Valley Metro light rail runs through campus
colleges.cost: Low to moderate cost of living
colleges.blurb: Flat desert grid, light rail on campus, and lower-than-average rent.

colleges.name: University of Colorado Boulder
colleges.walkability: Very bike-friendly, hilly at the edges
colleges.transit: RTD buses plus a free campus Buff Bus
colleges.blurb: One of the most bike-friendly cities in the US, but rent runs high.

colleges.name: University of Vermont
colleges.transit: GMT buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Small, walkable, lakeside city with a free city bus pass.

colleges.name: UMass Amherst
colleges.transit: PVTA buses free for all students
colleges.blurb: Free PVTA bus system connects the whole Five College area.

colleges.name: Michigan State University
colleges.walkability: Walkable core, sprawling at the edges
colleges.transit: CATA buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Free CATA bus pass covers East Lansing and Lansing both.

colleges.name: University of Iowa
colleges.transit: Free Cambus campus shuttle network
colleges.blurb: Free Cambus shuttle network and low rent make this an easy car-free pick.

colleges.name: Indiana University Bloomington
colleges.transit: Bloomington Transit free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Small walkable downtown, free city buses, and cheap rent.

colleges.name: Temple University
colleges.transit: SEPTA subway and regional rail nearby
colleges.cost: Moderate to high citywide, lower near campus
colleges.blurb: Subway access in a dense city, with cheaper rent near the North Philly campus.

colleges.name: Ohio State University
colleges.transit: COTA buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Free citywide bus pass; the campus area is dense enough to skip a car.

colleges.name: University of North Carolina
colleges.transit: Chapel Hill Transit, fare-free citywide
colleges.blurb: Fully fare-free public transit citywide, though rent runs above average.

colleges.name: University of Georgia
colleges.transit: Athens Transit free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Walkable core and free buses, but coverage thins out past downtown.

colleges.name: University of Texas at Austin
colleges.transit: CapMetro buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Campus itself is walkable, but Austin sprawls and rent is steep.

colleges.name: Rutgers University
colleges.transit: Free intercampus bus plus NJ Transit rail
colleges.blurb: Free bus links the spread-out campuses; NJ Transit reaches NYC and Philly.

colleges.name: University of Kansas
colleges.walkability: Walkable hill, town spreads out flat
colleges.transit: Free KU on Wheels campus bus
colleges.blurb: Cheap to live, but the town spreads out enough that a bike helps a lot.

colleges.name: Penn State
colleges.transit: CATA buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Walkable around campus, but State College sits in a rural valley.

colleges.name: Texas A&M University
colleges.transit: Aggie Spirit buses, limited evenings and weekends
colleges.blurb: Buses cover the basics, but the town is built for cars off-hours.

colleges.name: Auburn University
colleges.transit: Tiger Transit, campus-only buses
colleges.blurb: Cheap to live, but transit barely reaches past campus; expect to need a ride.

colleges.name: University of Alabama
colleges.transit: Crimson Ride, campus-only buses
colleges.blurb: Low cost of living, but almost no public transit beyond the campus shuttle.

colleges.name: Clemson University
colleges.transit: CATbus, limited routes
colleges.blurb: A rural campus town; most students who skip a car rely on rides from friends.

colleges.name: New York University
colleges.transit: Subway everywhere, 24/7
colleges.blurb: Owning a car here is a burden, not a help; the subway is unbeatable. Rent is the cost.

colleges.name: Boston University
colleges.transit: MBTA Green Line runs through campus, student pass
colleges.blurb: The T's Green Line stops on campus; a car is pure liability in Boston.

colleges.name: University of Pennsylvania
colleges.transit: SEPTA subway, trolley, and regional rail
colleges.blurb: Trolleys and subway at the edge of campus; West Philly rent beats center city.

colleges.name: Georgetown University
colleges.walkability: Walkable but no Metro in Georgetown
colleges.transit: DC buses and Circulator; Metro a bus ride away
colleges.blurb: Very walkable, dense DC, but Georgetown itself famously has no Metro stop.

colleges.name: Columbia University
colleges.transit: 1 train at the campus gates, 24/7 subway
colleges.blurb: Subway stop right at the gates; nobody here needs a car. Rent is the catch.

colleges.name: University of Chicago
colleges.transit: Free UGo shuttles plus CTA buses and Metra
colleges.cost: Moderate rent for a big city
colleges.blurb: Free university shuttles plus CTA; Hyde Park rent is reasonable for Chicago.

colleges.name: Northwestern University
colleges.transit: CTA Purple Line and Metra into Chicago
colleges.blurb: Purple Line and Metra link a walkable suburb straight into the city.

colleges.name: University of Michigan
colleges.transit: Free TheRide buses plus university shuttles
colleges.blurb: Free bus pass and a compact downtown make Ann Arbor easy without a car.

colleges.name: University of California, Berkeley
colleges.transit: BART plus AC Transit, student pass
colleges.blurb: BART and buses cover the Bay; a car is a parking headache. Rent is steep.

colleges.name: UCLA
colleges.walkability: Walkable campus in a car city
colleges.transit: Metro plus BruinBus; expanding rail
colleges.blurb: Campus is walkable and transit is improving, but LA still rewards a car.

colleges.name: San Francisco State University
colleges.transit: Muni Metro stop on campus, BART nearby
colleges.blurb: Muni light rail stops on campus; the whole city is transit-reachable.

colleges.name: University of Maryland
colleges.transit: Metro Green Line plus free Shuttle-UM
colleges.blurb: A Metro stop and free shuttles connect campus to all of DC.

colleges.name: George Washington University
colleges.transit: Foggy Bottom Metro on campus
colleges.blurb: A Metro station sits on campus in the heart of DC; cars are pointless here.

colleges.name: Tulane University
colleges.transit: Historic streetcar line at the campus edge
colleges.blurb: The St. Charles streetcar runs along campus into the French Quarter.

colleges.name: University of Utah
colleges.transit: TRAX light rail stops on campus, student pass
colleges.blurb: Light rail reaches campus and downtown; the bench location is hilly.

colleges.name: University of New Mexico
colleges.transit: ABQ Ride buses plus the ART rapid line
colleges.blurb: Cheap to live with a decent rapid bus line, but the city sprawls.

colleges.name: University of Cincinnati
colleges.transit: Metro buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Walkable urban campus and a free bus pass; some steep hills around it.

colleges.name: University of Louisville
colleges.transit: TARC buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Free buses and cheap rent, but Louisville is built mostly for cars.

colleges.name: University of Tennessee
colleges.transit: Free KAT trolleys plus campus T-Link
colleges.blurb: Free trolleys around campus and downtown, but hills and gaps remain.

colleges.name: University of Missouri
colleges.transit: Free campus shuttles, limited city buses
colleges.blurb: Compact campus and downtown, cheap rent, but thin off-campus transit.

colleges.name: University of Nebraska-Lincoln
colleges.transit: StarTran buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Flat and bikeable with a free bus pass, but service is basic.

colleges.name: University of Arizona
colleges.transit: Sun Link streetcar runs through campus
colleges.blurb: A streetcar links campus and downtown; flat and bike-friendly if hot.

colleges.name: Florida State University
colleges.transit: StarMetro buses free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Free buses and a walkable core, but the city stretches out.

colleges.name: University of Florida
colleges.transit: RTS buses free with student ID, frequent
colleges.blurb: A big free bus system and bike lanes; midtown is dense, the rest sprawls.

colleges.name: Virginia Tech
colleges.transit: Blacksburg Transit free, frequent on campus
colleges.blurb: Free, frequent buses around a walkable town set deep in the mountains.

colleges.name: University of Virginia
colleges.transit: Free UTS and CAT buses
colleges.blurb: Walkable Grounds with free transit, but rent runs high for the region.

colleges.name: University of Kentucky
colleges.transit: Free campus transit, limited Lextran service
colleges.blurb: Cheap and walkable on campus, but the city needs a car for much.

colleges.name: Purdue University
colleges.transit: CityBus free with student ID
colleges.blurb: Compact campus, free buses, cheap rent; a bike covers the gaps.

colleges.name: University of Connecticut
colleges.transit: Free campus shuttles, sparse regional buses
colleges.blurb: Self-contained campus in a rural town; little reaches beyond it without a car.

colleges.name: University of Arkansas
colleges.transit: Razorback Transit free, plus limited Ozark buses
colleges.blurb: Free campus buses and cheap rent, but hilly and car-oriented past campus.

colleges.name: Mississippi State University
colleges.transit: SMART Transit, limited routes
colleges.blurb: A rural town built around the car; transit barely covers the basics.

colleges.name: University of Oklahoma
colleges.transit: CART buses, limited evenings/weekends
colleges.blurb: Flat and cheap, but spread out with thin transit off peak hours.

colleges.name: Iowa State University
colleges.transit: CyRide buses free, one of the best college systems
colleges.blurb: CyRide is a famously good free bus system; flat, bikeable, and cheap.

colleges.name: Kansas State University
colleges.transit: aTa Bus, limited service
colleges.blurb: Cheap with a lively walkable district, but limited buses past it.

colleges.name: West Virginia University
colleges.transit: The PRT automated people-mover links campuses, free
colleges.blurb: A unique free automated people-mover connects the hilly campuses.

colleges.name: University of South Carolina
colleges.transit: Free Gamecock shuttles, limited COMET buses
colleges.blurb: Walkable, downtown-adjacent campus with free shuttles; the city is car-first.

colleges.name: North Carolina State University
colleges.transit: Free Wolfline buses plus GoRaleigh
colleges.blurb: Free Wolfline buses and a walkable core, with growing city transit.

colleges.name: University of Montana
colleges.transit: Mountain Line buses, fare-free
colleges.blurb: A compact, bikeable mountain town with a fully fare-free bus system.

colleges.name: University of Hawaii at Manoa
colleges.transit: TheBus, one of the most-used US systems; rail opening
colleges.blurb: Excellent buses and a new rail line, but Honolulu is very expensive.

colleges.name: University of Amsterdam
colleges.transit: Trams, metro, and bike lanes everywhere
colleges.cost: High rent, but a car is unthinkable here
colleges.blurb: Everyone bikes; the city is built for it. Rent is the only real cost.

colleges.name: Delft University of Technology
colleges.transit: Bikes plus frequent trains to Rotterdam/The Hague
colleges.blurb: A flat Dutch student town where a bike does everything.

colleges.name: Utrecht University
colleges.transit: Bikes plus a central rail hub
colleges.blurb: One of the most bike-friendly cities anywhere; great train links.

colleges.name: University of Copenhagen
colleges.transit: Metro, S-train, and cycle superhighways
colleges.blurb: A global cycling capital, but Copenhagen is expensive to live in.

colleges.name: University of Cambridge
colleges.transit: Bikes plus buses; fast train to London
colleges.blurb: A small, bike-and-walk university town; nobody needs a car.

colleges.name: University of Oxford
colleges.transit: Bikes and frequent buses; rail to London
colleges.blurb: Walk or cycle everywhere in a compact, ancient city.

colleges.name: University of Edinburgh
colleges.transit: Buses plus trams; very walkable centre
colleges.blurb: A walkable capital with good buses; the hills are the only effort.

colleges.name: University College London
colleges.transit: The Tube and buses, 24/7 on some lines
colleges.blurb: Unbeatable transit, but London rent is among the highest anywhere.

colleges.name: Trinity College Dublin
colleges.transit: Luas trams, DART, and buses
colleges.blurb: A walkable central campus with trams, but Dublin rent is steep.

colleges.name: Humboldt University
colleges.transit: U-Bahn, S-Bahn, trams; semester ticket included
colleges.blurb: Superb transit, a flat bikeable city, and rent that's low for a capital.

colleges.name: Technical University of Munich
colleges.transit: U-Bahn/S-Bahn plus a semester ticket
colleges.blurb: Excellent transit and the semester ticket, though Munich rent is high.

colleges.name: University of Vienna
colleges.transit: World-class U-Bahn/trams; cheap semester pass
colleges.blurb: Vienna's transit is world-class and the student pass is famously cheap.

colleges.name: Sorbonne University
colleges.transit: The Metro reaches everything
colleges.blurb: The Metro makes a car absurd, but central Paris rent is brutal.

colleges.name: University of Bologna
colleges.transit: Buses; very walkable centre
colleges.blurb: Europe's oldest university in a compact, walkable, arcaded city.

colleges.name: Lund University
colleges.transit: Bikes plus trains to Malmo/Copenhagen
colleges.blurb: A flat little student town where the bike rules.

colleges.name: Charles University
colleges.transit: Metro, trams, buses; cheap student pass
colleges.blurb: Great cheap transit and low rent make Prague a car-free bargain.

colleges.name: University of Barcelona
colleges.transit: Metro and buses across the city
colleges.blurb: A dense, walkable city with a deep metro network.

colleges.name: Ghent University
colleges.transit: Trams and bikes; centre is largely car-free
colleges.blurb: Ghent's centre is famously car-light; trams and bikes do the rest.

colleges.name: McGill University
colleges.transit: Metro plus buses; an underground city
colleges.blurb: Metro at the gates and rent that's low for a major North American city.

colleges.name: University of Toronto
colleges.transit: TTC subway and streetcars
colleges.blurb: Subway and streetcars at the door, but Toronto rent is high.

colleges.name: University of British Columbia
colleges.transit: Frequent express buses; SkyTrain nearby
colleges.blurb: Great bus service and a walkable campus, but Vancouver rent is extreme.

colleges.name: University of Tokyo
colleges.transit: The world's best rail/metro network
colleges.blurb: Tokyo's trains make a car pointless; rent is high but doable in shares.

colleges.name: Kyoto University
colleges.transit: Buses, subway, and bikes
colleges.blurb: A flat, bikeable city with good buses and lower rent than Tokyo.

colleges.name: National University of Singapore
colleges.transit: The MRT and buses reach everything
colleges.blurb: Owning a car is deliberately expensive here; the MRT is excellent.

colleges.name: University of Hong Kong
colleges.transit: The MTR is fast, cheap, and everywhere
colleges.blurb: The MTR is world-class; almost nobody students-age drives. Rent is the catch.

colleges.name: Seoul National University
colleges.transit: Seoul's vast cheap subway
colleges.blurb: One of the world's best subway systems, cheap and far-reaching.

colleges.name: University of Melbourne
colleges.transit: Trams (free in the CBD zone) and trains
colleges.blurb: Melbourne's tram network, free in the centre, makes car-free easy.

colleges.name: University of Sydney
colleges.transit: Trains, buses, and light rail
colleges.blurb: Good transit and a walkable campus, but Sydney rent is among the world's highest.

colleges.name: University of Auckland
colleges.transit: Buses and trains, improving but car-centric city
colleges.blurb: A walkable central campus, but Auckland still leans heavily on cars.

colleges.name: UNAM
colleges.transit: Metro and Metrobus, very cheap
colleges.blurb: A vast cheap metro and low rent; the city is dense and transit-rich.

colleges.name: University of Buenos Aires
colleges.transit: The Subte plus extensive buses
colleges.blurb: Walkable barrios and a cheap subway-and-bus network.

colleges.name: Education City (HBKU)
colleges.transit: Doha Metro Education City station + a campus tram
colleges.blurb: The new metro and a campus tram help, but Doha is hot and very car-oriented.

colleges.name: Qatar University
colleges.transit: Doha Metro red line nearby; limited local buses
colleges.blurb: A metro line helps, but the campus and city are built around the car and the heat.

colleges.name: American University of Beirut
colleges.transit: Shared vans and buses, informal; very walkable core
colleges.blurb: The Hamra area is walkable, but formal transit is thin and informal.

colleges.name: University of Cape Town
colleges.transit: Jammie campus shuttles plus MyCiTi buses
colleges.blurb: Free campus shuttles help, but the wider city leans on cars and the train is patchy.
